title: GatewayTransferNative onCall charges fee but still swaps gross amount, enabling reserve-subsidized payouts
reason: Already covered by F-001 (same root cause: fee deducted then swap logic still trusts attacker-controlled swap params/nominal amounts and can externalize deficits to reserves).

title: GatewayTransferNative revert callbacks can hard-revert on short revertMessage
reason: Low-value malformed-callback scenario; in protocol-generated flows this contract builds revert messages with at least 32 bytes, so this is not a realistic protocol-level exploit path.

title: SwapDataHelperLib decodeCompressedMixSwapParams lacks calldata bounds checks
reason: Claimed out-of-bounds stale-data read is unsupported; malformed inputs mainly cause revert/self-failure, not exploitable cross-user fund impact.

title: SwapDataHelperLib decodeCompressedMixSwapParams offset arithmetic can underflow
reason: No credible underflow path: offset is only incremented, never decremented.

title: GatewaySend onCall lacks reentrancy protection on token transfers
reason: `onCall` is `onlyGateway`; proposed reentrant entry is not realistically reachable by arbitrary recipients, and no concrete state-dependent drain path was shown.

title: GatewaySend onCall does not validate outputAmount >= amount for swap paths
reason: Not a protocol vulnerability by itself; swap slippage/price impact is governed by swap parameters (`minReturnAmount`) and market conditions.
